name: Networking Concepts
description: Foundational models and addressing: the OSI layers, common ports and protocols, IPv4/IPv6 addressing, and the building blocks that everything else builds on.

name: Network Implementation
description: Building the network: routing and switching behavior, VLANs, wireless standards, and the cabling and devices that connect endpoints.

name: Network Operations
description: Keeping the network healthy: documentation, monitoring and metrics, high availability, and the disaster-recovery concepts that limit downtime.

name: Network Security
description: Defending the network: security concepts, common attack types, hardening techniques, and the access controls that keep traffic and devices trustworthy.

name: Network Troubleshooting
description: Diagnosing faults methodically: a structured troubleshooting process plus the cabling, connectivity, and software tools used to isolate and fix problems.
